build.details: Build completed successfully with 3 warnings (pre-existing performance warnings, not related to changes)

testPageGate.details: Test page contains 12 instances of takt/masonry-cards block with comprehensive variations including text cards, image cards, tall cards, various column counts (2, 3, 4), masonry and aligned layouts

blockRegistration.details: ImageCard block.json correctly defines alt attribute with type string and default empty string. All required files exist.

accessibility.details: Critical TSX/PHP sync failure: role='listitem' not rendering in frontend HTML. Grid role='list' works correctly.

patternCompliance.details: role='list' on grid container is correct and renders in HTML. role='listitem' on cards is broken - attribute not appearing in rendered HTML despite being in PHP templates.

tsxPhpSync.details: Critical sync failure in card templates. TSX uses role in useBlockProps object, PHP attempts to add role as standalone attribute before theme_block_props() which creates invalid HTML syntax and causes role='listitem' to not render.

imageAltAttribute.details: ImageCard block.json defines alt attribute. ImageCard.tsx has TextControl in sidebar. ImageCard.php conditionally passes alt to wp_get_attachment_image. Implementation is correct.

issues.description: role='listitem' not rendering in frontend HTML for TextCard
issues.details: PHP template has `<div role="listitem"` on line 18 as a standalone attribute before the opening tag is closed, followed by theme_block_props() call, followed by standalone `>` on line 40. This creates invalid HTML syntax. The role attribute is not being passed to theme_block_props() and is therefore stripped or ignored by WordPress rendering.
issues.verification: The theme_block_props() function (inc/helpers/blocks.php line 55) accepts a second parameter $props for additional HTML attributes. On line 90-98, it merges $props into $args and passes them to get_block_wrapper_attributes(). This is the correct way to add custom attributes.

issues.description: role='listitem' not rendering in frontend HTML for ImageCard
issues.details: Identical issue to FQA-A11Y-001. PHP template has `<div role="listitem"` on line 19 as a standalone attribute before the opening tag is closed, followed by theme_block_props() call on line 21-39, followed by standalone `>` on line 40. This creates invalid HTML syntax and the role attribute does not render.
issues.alsoUpdate: Same pattern needed in TextCard.php line 18-40

roleListVerification.result: All 12 masonry-cards sections have role='list' on grid container (extra_selectors.grid_container_with_role_list.found: true for all sections)
roleListVerification.conclusion: PASS - grid container role works correctly

roleListitemVerification.conclusion: FAIL - role='listitem' not rendering in HTML

htmlSyntaxAnalysis.finding: PHP templates use invalid syntax: `<div role="listitem"` followed by theme_block_props() followed by `>`. This pattern does not pass the role attribute to theme_block_props() and results in malformed HTML where the role attribute is either stripped or ignored.
htmlSyntaxAnalysis.correctPattern: theme_block_props() accepts $props as second parameter (line 55 of inc/helpers/blocks.php). Custom attributes should be passed as [ 'role' => 'listitem' ] in the second parameter, not as standalone text before the function call.
htmlSyntaxAnalysis.reference: inc/helpers/blocks.php lines 90-98 show how $props are merged into $args and passed to get_block_wrapper_attributes()

imageAltTextVerification.blockJson: alt attribute defined in ImageCard/block.json line 29-32
imageAltTextVerification.phpRendering: Conditional alt passing in ImageCard.php lines 47-49
imageAltTextVerification.conclusion: PASS - alt text implementation is correct in all three layers

gridContainer.tsx: MasonryCards.tsx line 97: role: 'list' in useInnerBlocksProps first argument

textCard.tsx: TextCard.tsx line 80: role: 'listitem' in useBlockProps argument object
textCard.sync: FAIL - TSX passes role via useBlockProps object (correct), PHP attempts standalone attribute (incorrect, causes role to not render)

imageCard.tsx: ImageCard.tsx line 105: role: 'listitem' in useBlockProps argument object

tsxPhpSyncAnalysis.correctPHPPattern:
theme_block_props() accepts two parameters: $classes (array) and $props (array). Custom HTML attributes like role should be passed as $props, not as standalone text. Example:

<?php
theme_block_props(
	[ 'class-1' => true, 'class-2' => $condition ],
	[ 'role' => 'listitem', 'data-custom' => 'value' ]
);
?>
